# Project Solaris — Overview

Project Solaris is a renewable-energy initiative launched by Helios Labs in 2024.
Its primary goal is to develop next-generation perovskite solar cells with a
target efficiency of 32%, up from the current industry baseline of 24%.

Solaris is led by Dr. Mei Tanaka and operates out of the Helios Labs facility in
Tsukuba, Japan. The program runs on a three-year roadmap ending in 2027.
